text: The world feels muted and distant, the harsh angles evoking a sense of unease as I wander this dimly lit interior. I am unsettled yet curious, my presence almost spectral amid rigid lines and cold glass. When confronted with enigmatic signage and abstract displays, I am spurred into contemplation rather than comfort. The text on the wall challenges me to view this environment as an evolving experiment, prompting deep reflection. I sense an invitation to question, to wonder, and to search for meaning in the sterile quiet—standing here at the blurry intersection of thought and perception.
